XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerateGetResults

Retrieves leaderboard entries requested by the XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate function.

Note    Leaderboards are also referred to as scoreboards.

HRESULT XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerateGetResults(
    XONLINETASK_HANDLE hTask,
    DWORD dwUserCount,
    PXONLINE_STAT_USER pUsers,
    DWORD dwStatCount,
    PXONLINE_STAT pStats,
    DWORD *pdwLeaderboardSize,
    DWORD *pdwReturnedResults,
    DWORD dwExtraBufferSize,
    BYTE *pExtraBuffer
);

Parameters

hTask
[in] Handle to the task returned by the XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate function.
dwUserCount
[in] Number of XONLINE_STAT_USER structures pointed to by the pUsers parameter.
pUsers
[out] Points to an array of XONLINE_STAT_USER structures. The number of structures should be the same as the value specified by the dwPageSize parameter in the call to XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate.
dwStatCount
[in] Number of XONLINE_STAT structures pointed to by the pStats parameter.
pStats
[out] Pointer to an array of XONLINE_STAT structures that receives the retrieved statistics. The number of structures in this array should be equal to the number of players being retrieved from the leaderboard times the number of stats requested per player, that is, the product of the dwPageSize and dwNumStatsPerUser values that were passed in to the original XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate call.
pdwLeaderboardSize
[out] Pointer to a DWORD variable that receives the total number of players listed on the leaderboard that was specified in the call to XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate.
pdwReturnedResults
[out] Pointer to a DWORD variable that receives the number of players actually returned.
dwExtraBufferSize
[in] Size, in bytes, of the buffer pointed to by the pExtraBuffer parameter.
pExtraBuffer
[in, out] Pointer to a buffer that receives variable-length data associated with the retrieved statistics. See the Remarks section for more information.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful.

If the function fails, it returns one of the following error codes.

Value Description
E_INVALIDARG The specified buffer size is not valid. Check the specified buffer size.
E_FAIL Unspecified failure. Check your arguments to this function. If the arguments are correct, contact developer support. If this happens in the context of a game, try again later.
Other See Online Errors for information about other possible errors.

Remarks

The XOnlineStatReadGetResult and XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerateGetResults functions always return title-defined statistics sorted by the statistic ID. This order may be different than the order specified by the title in the call to XOnlineStatRead or XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate. The reserved attributes are always returned last. The reserved attributes are Rank, Rating, Leaderboard Size, and Nickname.

The buffer pointed to by the pExtraBuffer parameter receives variable-length data associated with the retrieved statistics. This buffer stores player nicknames, and statistics attachment paths. Use the following formula to calculate how much space to allocate for the buffer specified by the parameter dwExtraBufferSize.

= <number_of_requested_nicknames> * XONLINE_STAT_MAX_NICKNAME_LENGTH * sizeof(WCHAR)

+ <number_of_requested_attachment_paths> * XONLINESTORAGE_MAX_PATH * sizeof(WCHAR)

The title does not need to parse the data in the buffer; the lpString member of XONLINE_STAT points directly to the location of the string in the buffer.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

Xbox Live Functions | XOnlineStatLeaderEnumerate
